result.summary: The page is mostly a functional casino lobby, but there is one clear data/content bug (a negative jackpot amount) plus several layout truncation issues at the right edge and bottom of the screen that make content look cut off or broken.

issues.description: The 'Cop The Lot Megaways' game tile displays a negative jackpot value of '-$6,266.13', which is impossible for a jackpot and suggests a data or formatting bug.
issues.suggestion: Validate jackpot feed values before rendering; hide or show a fallback (e.g., 'Jackpot') when the value is negative or invalid.

issues.description: The last navigation chip in the top category bar is truncated to 'Hot Gar' (likely 'Hot Games'), and its pill is clipped at the right edge with no visual scroll affordance.
issues.suggestion: Add horizontal scroll indicators (fade/gradient or arrow) or adjust chip sizing so labels aren't cut off mid-word at the viewport edge.

issues.description: Game tiles in the right column of every carousel row are cut off at the screen edge (e.g., 'MAGNI...PHO', 'MUNC...MI', 'HYPERC...', 'DAR WATER'), which may be intentional peeking but looks abrupt without a scroll hint.
issues.suggestion: Show a consistent partial-tile peek width or add a right-edge gradient/arrow to signal horizontal scrollability.

issues.description: The bottom row of game tiles ('Millionaire', pig-themed slot, 'Dark Waters') is vertically clipped by the fixed bottom navigation bar, hiding titles and info buttons.
issues.suggestion: Add bottom padding equal to the fixed nav bar height so content can scroll fully into view above it.

issues.description: The 'Casino' item in the bottom navigation is styled as underlined text unlike the other uppercase labels, making the active state look inconsistent with the rest of the nav.
issues.suggestion: Use a consistent active-state treatment (e.g., color highlight matching the icon style) rather than an underlined, differently-cased label.
